---
title: "Migrate Special Activities"
date: "2026-03-01"
format:
  html:
    toc: true
    html-math-method: katex
    code-fold: false
    embed-resources: true
knitr:
  opts_chunk:
    echo: true
---


# Structure

## Create New Tables


```{sql}
--| eval: false

SET standard_conforming_strings = ON;
-- SET search_path TO pg_catalog,public,"inbound";

BEGIN;
DROP TABLE IF EXISTS "inbound"."InstallationVisits" CASCADE;

CREATE TABLE "inbound"."InstallationVisits"()
INHERITS ("inbound"."Visits")
;

COMMENT ON TABLE "inbound"."InstallationVisits" IS E'extra information collected for certain activities (well installations); subset of Visits';

ALTER TABLE "inbound"."InstallationVisits" ADD COLUMN installationvisit_id int NOT NULL; 
COMMENT ON COLUMN "inbound"."InstallationVisits".installationvisit_id IS E'technical index (extra, on top of visit_id)';
ALTER TABLE "inbound"."InstallationVisits" ADD COLUMN photo_soil_1_peilbuis varchar; 
COMMENT ON COLUMN "inbound"."InstallationVisits".photo_soil_1_peilbuis IS E'photo of the soil drill profile: peilbuis';
ALTER TABLE "inbound"."InstallationVisits" ADD COLUMN photo_soil_2_piezometer varchar; 
COMMENT ON COLUMN "inbound"."InstallationVisits".photo_soil_2_piezometer IS E'photo of the soil drill profile: piezometer';
ALTER TABLE "inbound"."InstallationVisits" ADD COLUMN photo_well varchar; 
COMMENT ON COLUMN "inbound"."InstallationVisits".photo_well IS E'photo of the placed well/pair';
ALTER TABLE "inbound"."InstallationVisits" ADD COLUMN watina_code_used_1_peilbuis varchar; 
COMMENT ON COLUMN "inbound"."InstallationVisits".watina_code_used_1_peilbuis IS E'watina code of the `odd` subset of wells';
ALTER TABLE "inbound"."InstallationVisits" ADD COLUMN watina_code_used_2_piezometer varchar; 
COMMENT ON COLUMN "inbound"."InstallationVisits".watina_code_used_2_piezometer IS E'watina code of the `even` subset of wells';
ALTER TABLE "inbound"."InstallationVisits" ADD COLUMN soilprofile_notes varchar; 
COMMENT ON COLUMN "inbound"."InstallationVisits".soilprofile_notes IS E'notes about the soil profiles';
ALTER TABLE "inbound"."InstallationVisits" ADD COLUMN soilprofile_unclear boolean NOT NULL DEFAULT FALSE; 
COMMENT ON COLUMN "inbound"."InstallationVisits".soilprofile_unclear IS E'help requested for evaluation of the soil profile';
ALTER TABLE "inbound"."InstallationVisits" ADD COLUMN random_point_number smallint; 
COMMENT ON COLUMN "inbound"."InstallationVisits".random_point_number IS E'if so, which random point was chosen';
ALTER TABLE "inbound"."InstallationVisits" ADD COLUMN no_diver boolean NOT NULL DEFAULT FALSE; 
COMMENT ON COLUMN "inbound"."InstallationVisits".no_diver IS E'highlight issues (use notes to specify)';
ALTER TABLE "inbound"."InstallationVisits" ADD COLUMN diver_id varchar; 
COMMENT ON COLUMN "inbound"."InstallationVisits".diver_id IS E'link to the table of available divers';
ALTER TABLE "inbound"."InstallationVisits" ADD COLUMN free_diver varchar; 
COMMENT ON COLUMN "inbound"."InstallationVisits".free_diver IS E'free field to enter a non-registered diver code';
ALTER TABLE "inbound"."InstallationVisits" ADD COLUMN reused_existing_well boolean DEFAULT FALSE; 
COMMENT ON COLUMN "inbound"."InstallationVisits".reused_existing_well IS E'flag if an existing installation was reused';
ALTER TABLE "inbound"."InstallationVisits" ADD COLUMN reused_with_replacement boolean DEFAULT FALSE; 
COMMENT ON COLUMN "inbound"."InstallationVisits".reused_with_replacement IS E'whether an existing installation was reused and refreshed';
ALTER TABLE "inbound"."InstallationVisits" ADD COLUMN used_water_from_tap boolean; 
COMMENT ON COLUMN "inbound"."InstallationVisits".used_water_from_tap IS E'whether or not tap water was used for installation';
ALTER TABLE "inbound"."InstallationVisits" ADD COLUMN used_water_source varchar; 
COMMENT ON COLUMN "inbound"."InstallationVisits".used_water_source IS E'source of the water used for facilitating installation';

COMMIT;

-- sequence installationvisit_id
CREATE SEQUENCE "inbound".seq_installationvisit_id
INCREMENT BY 1
MINVALUE 0
MAXVALUE 2147483647
START WITH 1
CACHE 1
NO CYCLE
OWNED BY "inbound"."InstallationVisits".installationvisit_id;
ALTER TABLE "inbound"."InstallationVisits" ALTER COLUMN installationvisit_id
 SET DEFAULT nextval('inbound.seq_installationvisit_id'::regclass);

GRANT USAGE ON SEQUENCE "inbound"."seq_installationvisit_id" TO tom, yglinga, jens, lise, wouter, floris, karen, janne, falk, ward, monkey;
GRANT SELECT ON SEQUENCE "inbound"."seq_installationvisit_id" TO monkey;

GRANT SELECT ON "inbound"."InstallationVisits" TO tom, yglinga, jens, lise, wouter, floris, karen, janne, falk, ward, monkey;

GRANT INSERT ON "inbound"."InstallationVisits" TO tom, yglinga, jens, lise, wouter, floris, karen, janne, falk;
GRANT UPDATE ON "inbound"."InstallationVisits" TO tom, yglinga, jens, lise, wouter, floris, karen, janne, falk;
GRANT DELETE ON "inbound"."InstallationVisits" TO tom, yglinga, jens, lise, wouter, floris, karen, janne, falk;


```


## Fill With Data

this is somewhat more complex;
I need to capture the data before DELETE FROM ONLY "inbound"."Visits" ;
ore move the rows with INSERT... SELECT FROM ONLY;
but all that while respecting uniqueness constraints.
Maybe better have an R script?

```{sql}
--| eval: false
-- BEGIN;

INSERT INTO "inbound"."InstallationVisits" (
  log_user,
  log_update,
  samplelocation_id,
  fieldworkcalendar_id,
  grts_address,
  stratum,
  activity_group_id,
  date_start,
  photo_soil_1_peilbuis,
  photo_soil_2_piezometer,
  photo_well,
  watina_code_used_1_peilbuis,
  watina_code_used_2_piezometer,
  soilprofile_notes,
  soilprofile_unclear,
  random_point_number,
  no_diver,
  diver_id,
  free_diver,
  reused_existing_well,
  reused_with_replacement,
  used_water_from_tap,
  used_water_source
)
SELECT
  WIA.log_user,
  WIA.log_update,
  VIS.samplelocation_id,
  VIS.fieldworkcalendar_id,
  VIS.grts_address,
  VIS.stratum,
  VIS.activity_group_id,
  VIS.date_start,
  WIA.photo_soil_1_peilbuis,
  WIA.photo_soil_2_piezometer,
  WIA.photo_well,
  WIA.watina_code_used_1_peilbuis,
  WIA.watina_code_used_2_piezometer,
  WIA.soilprofile_notes,
  WIA.soilprofile_unclear,
  WIA.random_point_number,
  WIA.no_diver,
  WIA.diver_id,
  WIA.free_diver,
  WIA.reused_existing_well,
  WIA.reused_with_replacement,
  WIA.used_water_from_tap,
  WIA.used_water_source
FROM "inbound"."WellInstallationActivities" AS WIA, "inbound"."Visits" AS VIS
WHERE VIS.visit_id = WIA.visit_id
  AND VIS.grts_address = WIA.grts_address
  AND VIS.stratum = WIA.stratum
  AND VIS.date_start = WIA.date_start
  AND VIS.activity_group_id = 4
  AND WIA.activity_group_id = 4
;


```


## Redirect Traffic

<https://stackoverflow.com/questions/12343984/insert-trigger-to-update-another-table-using-postgresql>

All updates on the old table should be reflected in the new one.
Users do not do inserts etc.


```{sql}
--| eval: false

DROP FUNCTION IF EXISTS "metadata".sync_wia CASCADE;
CREATE FUNCTION "metadata".sync_wia() RETURNS trigger AS $sync_wia$
BEGIN

  UPDATE "inbound"."InstallationVisits"
  SET 
      photo_soil_1_peilbuis = NEW.photo_soil_1_peilbuis,
      photo_soil_2_piezometer = NEW.photo_soil_2_piezometer,
      photo_well = NEW.photo_well,
      watina_code_used_1_peilbuis = NEW.watina_code_used_1_peilbuis,
      watina_code_used_2_piezometer = NEW.watina_code_used_2_piezometer,
      soilprofile_notes = NEW.soilprofile_notes,
      soilprofile_unclear = NEW.soilprofile_unclear,
      random_point_number = NEW.random_point_number,
      no_diver = NEW.no_diver,
      diver_id = NEW.diver_id,
      free_diver = NEW.free_diver,
      reused_existing_well = NEW.reused_existing_well,
      reused_with_replacement = NEW.reused_with_replacement,
      used_water_from_tap = NEW.used_water_from_tap,
      used_water_source = NEW.used_water_source
  WHERE grts_address = NEW.grts_address
    AND stratum = NEW.stratum
    AND activity_group_id = 4
    AND date_start = NEW.date_start
  ;
  
  RETURN NEW;
END;
$sync_wia$ LANGUAGE plpgsql;

DROP TRIGGER IF EXISTS wia_sync_trigger ON "inbound"."WellInstallationActivities";
CREATE TRIGGER wia_sync_trigger
BEFORE UPDATE ON "inbound"."WellInstallationActivities"
FOR EACH ROW EXECUTE PROCEDURE "metadata".sync_wia();

-- COMMIT;
```
